rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JERROLD HARATANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/05/2020. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The supplement alleges that "an inventory and appraisal is done to identify the assets of the conservatee at the start of the conservatorship", but Probate Code § 2613 requires a supplemental inventory and appraisal not only whenever any property of the conservatee is discovered that was not included in the inventory, but also whenever any other property is received by the conservatee or conservator on behalf of the conservatee. The appraisal must be made as of the date the property was received. 2) The supplement alleges that the Wells Fargo CD in IRA account x6366 matured during the account period, but does not explain why this account is not listed on Schedule 1. Schedule 1 only shows the Wells Fargo IRA account x2689. 3) The supplement alleges that the legal fees of $937.97 paid to the petitioner's attorneys on 12/01/2017 were authorized by the prior court order dated 08/08/2017, but the amount ordered was $6,873.49 and fully paid on 08/17/2017 according to the first account. 4) The supplement does not address the issue of whether the court's 07/23/2018 order approved the reimbursement of costs of $665.22 to the petitioner's attorneys, as so reported on Schedule C-1. 5) The supplement does not itemize the additional 20.727 shares of Fidelity Growth Strategies Fund acquired during the account period. According to the account statement, 15.102 shares were purchased by dividend reinvestment on 12/27/2019, but the purchase(s) of the remaining 5.626 shares were not explained in the account. The account only identifies receipts of dividends reinvested. *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARIA MIYAMOTO AND CECILIA HARATANI LIVING TRUST DATED JUNE 6, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/05/2020. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The supplement alleges that the receipts and disbursements do not need to be allocated between principal and income because the Jerrold Haratani is entitled to receive both income and principal, but does not explain why CRC 7.901(c) should not apply when a beneficiary of an irrevocable trust is entitled to mandatory net income and limited discretionary principal during his lifetime. 2) The supplement alleges that the petitioner paid 6-month management fees of $5,220 on 12/07/2018 and 08/19/2019 in reliance on the court's 07/23/2018 order which authorized fees on account for the petitioner's services up to 3 hours per month at a rate of $145 for a total payment of $435 per month, but the total of authorized fees on account for the petitioner's services for 6 months is $2,610. Did the petitioner make advance payments for her future services? 3) No explanation has been provided from Alan Sparer as to why 100% of his firm's fees and costs were allocated to the trust and 0% to the conservatorship. Does Alan Sparer's firm intend to seek additional fees and costs for the conservatorship? *Please note that appearances of both the petitioner and Sparer Law Group's attorney will be required at the hearing on the issue of fees and costs paid without prior court approval. *The petitioner and her attorneys need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings including account statements e-filed in this proceeding (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed). *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery Set 2
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Compel Further Responses to Written Discovery Set 2 - Sharon Browne Nassiri is granted in part and denied in part. While the Court agrees with Petitioner's time calculation of when this motion was due to be noticed, there is no showing that the responses at issue were verified and code-compliant under CCP sections 2030.299 and 2030.300, respectively. Both statutes calculate notice of the motion from service of verified responses. Sexton v. Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal. App. 4th 1403 relied on prior statutes, which did not contain the "verified" language. Respondent is not correct that Article 1 section 28 abrogates Evidence Code 787 in civil cases. Under section 28(f), "Any prior felony conviction of any person in any criminal proceeding, whether adult or juvenile, shall subsequently be used without limitation for purposes of impeachment or enhancement of sentence in any criminal proceeding [....]." People v. Dalton (2019) 7 Cal. 5th 166, 213-14 analyzed section 28's effect within a criminal case context. Section 28(f) does not apply to civil cases (Robbins v. Wong (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 261, 274). A witness' credibility may be impeached with evidence of a prior felony conviction in a civil case, subject to the court's powers under Evidence Code section 352. However, admissibility at trial is not the test of whether discovery is proper. Under CCP section 2017.010, "[A]ny party may obtain discovery regarding any matter, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action or to the determination of any motion made in that action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery or of any other party to the action." The motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted as to the disputed Requests for Admission and Form Interrogatories pertaining to Ms. Nassiri being charged with a felony, or entering a plea to a lesser felony charge, either of which may be reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence for the purposes of impeaching Ms. Nassiri's testimony as a witness at trial. The motion is denied as to the Requests for Admission and Form Interrogatories pertaining to locations and institutions in which Ms. Nassiri may have been detained. Discovery about federal charges or pleadings to federal charges may lead to relevant discovery about federal convictions, but where a person was held in custody does not appear reasonably calculated to lead to admissible impeachment evidence of a conviction. The Court further notes that Ms. Nassiri acknowledges in her declaration in support of the opposition to this motion she may have been convicted of a felony in the 1970's, and seemed willing to attempt to further answer Form Interrogatory 2.8 after the relevant court records departments resumed operations sufficiently to allow such research, but Respondent denied her request for an extension of time to respond. The parties are encouraged to further meet and confer in light of this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 21, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Petitioners To Require Trustee To Post Bond
rulings.Rulings: The Motion of Petitioners to Require Trustee to Post Bond is granted, pursuant to Probate Code 15602(a)(2). Notwithstanding that the current existence of the Portside 503 Trust is disputed in this matter and will not be determined on this motion, Petitioners have alleged that they have a present interest as beneficiaries of the Portside 503 Trust to a distribution of $100,000 each, upon their father's death, and are otherwise contingent beneficiaries. Respondent has not disputed in the motion Petitioners' assertion that the Portside 503 Trust existed at some point in time, or that the Petitioners were entitled to such a distribution upon Decedent's death. The parties do not otherwise appear to dispute in the motion papers that (1) under the Portside 503 Trust, Petitioners were contingent beneficiaries, due to receive whatever remained of the Trust corpus at Respondent's death, and (2) Respondent was not restricted from consuming the entirety of the Trust corpus during her lifetime following Decedent's death, minus the initial $100,000 distribution to each of the Petitioners. Thus, the only non-contingent interests Petitioners have shown in this motion which necessitate protection pursuant to Probate Code 15602(a)(2) are their interests in the initial $100,000 distributions. Respondent is ordered to post bond of $200,000, plus costs of bond. The cost of the bond shall be charged against the trust. Respondent's opposition to the motion was untimely. Although Petitioners were able to file a substantive reply within the required period, going forward, the parties should anticipate that the court will require compliance with the relevant rules and code provisions. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 21, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HERBERT DAN LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/05/2020. 1) Item 4 of notice of petition to administer estate was not checked. Amended notice must be given. 2) A proof of publication does not contain the language of item 4 of notice of petition to administer estate. Amended notice must be published. 3) If the petitioner is the sole trustee of the devisee trust, notice must be given to beneficiaries of the trust pursuant to Probate Code § 1208(b). 4) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Item 3d(1) alleges that the estate's personal property value is $0, even though annual income from personal property is $700,000. B) The self-proving box at item 3f(2) was not checked. C) Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the sole trustee, the name, age, and relationship to the decedent of each beneficiary entitled to notice under Probate Code § 1208(b) must be alleged. D) What are the names of the decedent's parents and dates of their death? *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom.
